architecture.summary: Ren - A ComfyUI AI assistant that provides natural language control over workflows. Multi-tier architecture: Python backend (FastAPI + PydanticAI) communicates via WebSocket with JavaScript frontend (ComfyUI extension). MCP subprocess handles tool execution, frontend executes workflow manipulation tools, PWA provides mobile access.

backend_server.description: FastAPI server managing WebSocket connections, agent orchestration, and session management

agent_system.description: PydanticAI-based agent with conversation management and MCP server integration

mcp_server.description: FastMCP tool server running as subprocess, provides 45+ tools for workflow control

session_manager.description: Multi-client session management with connection tracking per type (frontend/pwa/mcp)

callback_router.description: Routes tool execution callbacks between backend and frontend (legacy support)

comfy_tools.description: ComfyUI filesystem access tools for browsing custom nodes, models, and resources

node_library.description: ComfyUI node type discovery and compatibility checking via API

comfy_manager.description: ComfyUI Manager integration for node pack and model discovery

server_runner.description: Auto-start backend server when ComfyUI loads with monitoring and restart

comfyui_extension.description: ComfyUI extension entry point that auto-starts backend and registers web directory

frontend_extension.description: Main JavaScript extension coordinating WebSocket, tools, and UI

websocket_client.description: Frontend WebSocket client with event emitter pattern and reconnection logic

fl_api.description: Clean wrapper around legacy FL_JS functions for workflow manipulation

tool_executor.description: Executes tool requests from backend using FL_API, routes results back

chat_ui.description: Chat interface with markdown rendering, tool activity breadcrumbs, and Ren links

session_manager_frontend.description: Frontend session ID management using localStorage

query_executor.description: Client-side query DSL execution for workflow analysis

diagram_generator.description: Mermaid diagram generation from workflow structure

pwa_app.description: Progressive Web App for mobile access with session picker

legacy_fl_js.description: Original FL_JS implementation (still used for some tool execution)

relationships.relationship: Backend server creates agent instances per session via AgentManager, manages connections via ConnectionManager
Agent spawns MCP server as subprocess, communicates via WebSocket for tool execution
MCP subprocess sends tool_request to backend, backend routes to frontend via WebSocket
Frontend extension initializes WSClient which connects to backend WebSocket endpoint
ToolExecutor receives tool_request events, executes via FL_API, sends results back via WSClient
ChatUI sends user messages via WSClient, receives agent_response and tool_report events
MCP tools use ComfyUITools for filesystem, NodeLibraryClient for node discovery, ComfyManagerClient for pack search
Server loads Settings on startup, applies provider-specific tuning for LLM interactions
ComfyUI __init__.py uses ServerRunner to auto-start backend server on load
PWA connects to backend via WebSocket, can join existing frontend sessions
QueryExecutor uses FL_API to access workflow graph for filtering and traversal
CallbackRouter (legacy) provides alternative tool execution path, still used by some MCP tools

last_change: added tool_request and tool_report handlers to web/pwa/app.js
